sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports headaches that have been worsening lately, with a recent peak at a 7/10 severity after taking Tylenol, which reduces it to a 4-5. He admits to occasionally forgetting to take his lisinopril medication, particularly on weekends. He reports blood pressure readings of 160/87 and 175/92. He has noticed some congestion and allergies, but denies a runny nose or cough. His blood sugar has been running between 120 and 120. He reports urinating normally.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure readings were 160/87 and 175/92. Frontal and maxillary sinus tenderness are absent. No lymphadenopathy is palpated.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms are consistent with a headache, hypertension, and possible underlying diabetes management needs.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Increase lisinopril to 40mg once daily. Order routine blood work including hemoglobin, A1c, and a complete metabolic panel. Schedule diabetic education. Follow up to assess blood pressure control and blood sugar management.
